title: Maintenance, Commit Graphs, Index Acceleration, and Bundles · Independent Repository Trial
summary: An independent maintainer receives a different graph, repository policy, and failure constraint and must transfer maintenance, commit graphs, index acceleration, and bundles into a new measured maintenance, acceleration, and backup schedule. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of measured maintenance, acceleration, and backup schedule with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Explain gc, repack, cruft packs, prune, expiration, concurrent writers, keep files, geometric repack, and why manual cleanup needs reachability proof.
Configure incremental-repack, loose-objects, commit-graph, prefetch, gc, worktree, rerere-gc, and scheduler strategy from measured need.
Explain commit-graph generations, changed-path Bloom filters, split chains, multi-pack indexes, bitmaps, and compatibility or invalidation boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — Case G73 is a lab in a disposable repository. measured history walks and pack fanout justify two incremental tasks without immediate pruning. The learner must predict commit graph and pack layout update while reachable objects and active writers remain protected, run git maintenance run --task=commit-graph --task=incremental-repack, explain history latency drops and connectivity remains valid with no unreachable-object deletion, test this changed condition: introduce a concurrent writer and defer destructive expiration while retaining maintenance logs, and preserve this recovery boundary: verify a fresh bundle restore and preserve previous pack indexes before manual repair.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain gc, repack, cruft packs, prune, expiration, concurrent writers, keep files, geometric repack, and why manual cleanup needs reachability proof.

# Evidence: gita-bundle-backup-verification-1ta5lg
# scenario-key: trace-1ta5lg probe-gl5at1 boundary-a5lg1t owner-1ta5lgx fault-1ta5lgz
# before-ref: main=a11ce1t candidate=b00b1lg
# hypothesis: measured history walks and pack fanout justify two incremental tasks without immediate pruning

# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
